Source_Species.reason: The paper states transformation with the Petunia chi-a gene and experimental protocol specifies Petunia hybrida.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Experimental protocol: 'The Petunia chi gene was amplified ... from P. hybrida inbred line V30'.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Experimental protocol explicitly says 'P. hybrida inbred line V30'.

Applied_Species.reason: The study repeatedly states tomato was transformed and analyzed.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper says tomato/var. FM6203 but does not use the Latin name Solanum lycopersicum.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Experimental protocol: tomato '(var. FM6203, Unilever commercial variety)'.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Results and methods specify the gene was under the 'constitutive cauliflower mosaic virus (CaMV) double 35S promoter'.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper states the Petunia chi-a gene encodes chalcone isomerase.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is given anywhere in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper does not mention a 'Chalcone isomerase family' or domain classification.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Figure 1 and text explicitly refer to the flavonoid biosynthetic pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The study focuses on increasing flavonol biosynthesis; CHI is discussed as involved in flavonol biosynthesis and end products are flavonols.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Text states naringenin chalcone is converted into naringenin by CHI, placing it upstream of flavonols in the pathway.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Results: 'Naringenin chalcone represents an intermediate ... and is converted into the next compound in the pathway, naringenin, by the enzyme chalcone isomerase'.

Primary_Product.reason: Same passage explicitly identifies naringenin as the product of the CHI-catalyzed step.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Directly supported by the statement that CHI converts naringenin chalcone to naringenin.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper presents naringenin chalcone/naringenin as pathway intermediates leading to flavonol end products, so CHI forms the immediate precursor to downstream flavonols.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Results state that low chi transcript with high naringenin chalcone accumulation 'suggested that one of the rate-limiting steps in flavonol biosynthesis in peel could lie at the level of chi gene expression'.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results identify rutin as the major quercetin glycoside and also report quercetin glycosides and kaempferol glycosides/rutinoside accumulation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The end products are clearly flavonols, which are a subclass of flavonoids. Calling the class simply 'flavonoid' is broadly true but less precise than the paper's wording.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Intro discusses flavonols as potent antioxidants and cites epidemiological data suggesting reduced cardiovascular disease risk with high flavonoid intake.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results show accumulation in fruit peel; processing section says flavonols in peel leach into paste and are retained in processed paste.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results state low chi transcript in peel at all stages in high-flavonol transgenics; relatively high in leaves and in green, breaker, and turning flesh of pBBC50 plants; endogenous chi was below detection in peel and transcripts of chs/chi/fls undetectable in flesh.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The paper includes glasshouse growth, open field trial, fruit developmental stages, and processing into paste.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described in Results/methods: constitutive overexpression, segregation over generations, Northern blot, HPLC profiling, field trial, and processing tests.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper reports up to 78-fold increased peel flavonols mainly due to rutin/quercetin glycosides, increased kaempferol, and severe depletion of naringenin chalcone in high-flavonol transformants.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results mention no gross phenotypic differences, slight dullness of ripe fruit red tone, unchanged lycopene, and indistinguishable taste/flavor of paste.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Supported by stable inheritance over generations, elevated flavonols in field trials, retention in paste, and framing as functional food raw material.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each summary point matches reported findings: 66-fold quercetin and 57-fold kaempferol increases in hydrolyzed peel, overall up to 78-fold peel flavonols, rutin/isoquercitrin/kaempferol glycosides accumulation with naringenin chalcone depletion, cosegregation/stability through fourth generation and field trials, and ~65% retention in paste.

Other_Important_Info.reason: All points are present as author interpretations/discussion in Results: low endogenous chi may limit peel flavonol biosynthesis, rhamnosyltransferase may become limiting due to isoquercitrin accumulation, and possible indirect CHI effects are proposed but not demonstrated.

corrections.reason: The paper says tomato/var. FM6203 but does not use the Latin name Solanum lycopersicum.
No EC number is given anywhere in the paper.
The paper does not mention a 'Chalcone isomerase family' or domain classification.
